headline: Iran's Larijani Condemns Trump, Signals Nuclear Talks via Oman
summary: Ali Larijani, Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, publicly condemned Trump's regional policy while simultaneously pursuing backchannel nuclear negotiations via Omani mediators. Larijani is managing Iran's security posture following the death of Supreme Leader Khamenei, though he is not formally part of the interim ruling council. The dual-track approach — military retaliation plus diplomatic outreach — signals Iran is seeking an off-ramp while maintaining escalatory pressure.

secondary_markets.name: Brent Crude Oil
secondary_markets.reason: Any credible nuclear diplomacy signal reduces the probability of a full-scale Iran oil embargo, capping the geopolitical risk premium in crude prices.

secondary_markets.name: Gold (XAU/USD)
secondary_markets.reason: Post-Khamenei leadership vacuum combined with mixed military-diplomatic signals sustains safe-haven demand for gold as uncertainty premium remains elevated.

secondary_markets.name: TotalEnergies
secondary_markets.reason: A diplomatic resolution pathway could eventually reopen Iranian energy assets to European majors, with TotalEnergies holding historic Iranian upstream interests.

secondary_markets.name: Oman sovereign bonds
secondary_markets.reason: Oman's mediator role elevates its geopolitical standing and reduces its own conflict-spillover risk, supporting Omani sovereign credit spreads.

secondary_markets.name: iShares MSCI Emerging Markets ETF
secondary_markets.reason: Unresolved Iranian leadership succession combined with active regional conflict keeps risk-off pressure on EM assets broadly.

trade_position: Long gold (XAU/USD) spot as the primary safe-haven instrument — the Larijani dual-track signals that Iran's leadership transition is unstable and that military escalation will continue regardless of diplomatic rhetoric, sustaining the geopolitical risk premium. The hidden opportunity is that Omani mediation failure (likely given simultaneous Iranian missile strikes on Qatar and Kuwait) will be the catalyst for gold to break above recent resistance as nuclear risk premium reprices.

messages.text_en:
#Iran #USA

Ali Larijani, the Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, criticized former President Trump for causing instability in the region due to what he described as "delusional ambitions." He accused Trump of attempting to weaken Iran and sow discord among its people.

messages.text_en:
#Iran #USA

President Trump claimed that he preemptively struck Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei, stating, "I got him before he got me," referring to alleged Iranian plots against him. This statement was made following a successful U.S.-Israeli operation that resulted in Khamenei's death.
